sourceMovie.title: The Little Things
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Little Things (2021) stuck because it commits to its drama premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. John Lee Hancock's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The drama and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: American Psycho
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Little Things clicked for you, American Psycho (2000) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Little Things clicked for you, Collateral (2004) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Little Things?
faq.answer: Try American Psycho, Collateral, The Talented Mr. Ripley—each shares DNA with The Little Things while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Little Things?
faq.answer: The Little Things (2021) is best described as Drama, Crime, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Little Things worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
